name: Coffeyville Community College
notes: One of Kansas's top JUCO soccer programs with a strong national recruiting presence and history of producing transfers to D1/D2 programs.

name: Garden City Community College
notes: Active recruiting program in western Kansas; draws international players and is a strong KJCCC competitor.

name: Labette Community College
notes: Small Kansas JUCO with competitive soccer; good stepping stone to four-year programs in the Midwest.

name: Neosho County Community College
notes: KJCCC member with solid men's and women's soccer programs; known for developing players from rural Kansas and international pipelines.

name: Independence Community College
notes: Competing in KJCCC; recruits internationally and has a track record of sending players to four-year programs.

name: Allen Community College
notes: Strong KJCCC soccer program with a history of NJCAA tournament appearances and a notable international recruiting pipeline.

name: Butler Community College
notes: One of the larger Kansas JUCOs; solid soccer program with strong community support and good transfer outcomes.

name: Colby Community College
notes: Western Kansas JUCO competing in KJCCC; recruits regionally and internationally for its soccer program.

name: Pratt Community College
notes: Small rural KJCCC school with an active soccer program; good option for players seeking playing time and development.

name: Dodge City Community College
notes: KJCCC competitor with a growing soccer program; draws players from the greater southwest Kansas region and internationally.

name: Mineral Area College
notes: Well-regarded Missouri JUCO soccer program; regularly produces transfers to D2 and D3 programs throughout the Midwest.

name: Three Rivers College
notes: Competitive NJCAA soccer program in southeast Missouri; known for strong coaching and an active recruiting network.

name: Jefferson College
notes: St. Louis metro-area JUCO with a solid soccer tradition; good academics and transfer pathways to Missouri universities.

name: St. Louis Community College
notes: Large urban community college system with soccer programs competing in NJCAA; draws talent from the diverse St. Louis soccer community.

name: Iowa Central Community College
notes: One of Iowa's strongest JUCO soccer programs; recruits internationally and has a history of NJCAA tournament competition.

name: Iowa Western Community College
notes: Consistently competitive Iowa JUCO soccer program near the Omaha metro; known for international recruiting and high transfer rates to D1/D2.

name: Des Moines Area Community College
notes: Largest community college in Iowa; soccer program benefits from the strong Des Moines metro youth soccer scene.

name: Kirkwood Community College
notes: Well-funded Iowa JUCO with solid athletics; soccer program recruits from the eastern Iowa corridor.

name: Heartland Community College
notes: Central Illinois JUCO with an active soccer program; benefits from proximity to Illinois State University and a strong local soccer culture.

name: College of DuPage
notes: One of the largest community colleges in the US; competitive soccer program in the Chicago suburbs drawing from a strong youth soccer market.

name: Harper College
notes: Northwest Chicago suburban JUCO with a well-supported soccer program; strong academic transfer pipeline to Illinois four-year schools.

name: Joliet Junior College
notes: Historic Illinois JUCO (founded 1901) with a competitive soccer program; strong soccer tradition in the south Chicago suburbs.

name: Morton College
notes: West suburban Chicago JUCO with a strong soccer culture; heavily draws from the large Latino soccer community in Cicero and Berwyn.

name: Waubonsee Community College
notes: Fox Valley area Illinois JUCO with competitive soccer; serves the growing Aurora/Naperville corridor soccer community.

name: Elgin Community College
notes: Northwest suburban Chicago JUCO with an active men's and women's soccer program; draws from a diverse, soccer-rich recruiting area.

name: South Suburban College
notes: South Chicago suburban JUCO with soccer programs; draws from the strong south suburban Illinois and northwest Indiana soccer markets.

name: Highland Community College
notes: Northwest Illinois JUCO with a competitive soccer program; a solid development option for players in the greater Rockford area.

name: Parkland College
notes: Located in the University of Illinois college town; soccer program benefits from a rich athletic culture and serves as a bridge to Big Ten institutions.

name: Kishwaukee College
notes: Northern Illinois JUCO between Chicago and Rockford; competitive soccer program that draws from multiple suburban markets.

name: Monroe College
notes: Nationally elite NJCAA soccer program in the Bronx; multiple national championships and a pipeline of international talent. One of the top JUCO programs in the country.

name: Nassau Community College
notes: Large Long Island JUCO with a competitive soccer program; draws from a deep suburban New York talent pool and sends players to D1/D2 programs.

name: Suffolk Community College
notes: One of the largest community colleges in New York; soccer program draws from the strong Long Island youth soccer infrastructure.

name: Monroe Community College
notes: Rochester-area JUCO with a strong athletic tradition; soccer program benefits from the rich western New York soccer market.

name: Westchester Community College
notes: Draws from one of the wealthiest and most soccer-active counties in the US; competitive NJCAA soccer program close to New York City.

name: Dutchess Community College
notes: Mid-Hudson Valley JUCO with an active soccer program; draws from a strong Hudson Valley high school soccer market.

name: Brookdale Community College
notes: Monmouth County NJ JUCO with competitive soccer; draws from a dense, soccer-active population in central New Jersey.

name: Middlesex College
notes: Central New Jersey JUCO in a diverse, soccer-rich county; men's and women's programs consistently draw strong local and international talent.

name: County College of Morris
notes: Morris County NJ JUCO with a strong soccer program; competes in one of New Jersey's most competitive NJCAA regions.

name: Ocean County College
notes: Shore-area New Jersey JUCO with an active soccer program; draws from the large Ocean County youth soccer market.

name: Cecil College
notes: Northeast Maryland JUCO with a well-regarded soccer program; draws from the strong Delaware/Maryland border region soccer talent pool.

name: Montgomery College
notes: Large suburban DC JUCO with competitive soccer; draws from one of the most diverse and soccer-rich counties in the mid-Atlantic.

name: Prince George's Community College
notes: DC-area JUCO with strong soccer; benefits from the immense Central American soccer talent pool in Prince George's County, MD.

name: Northern Virginia Community College
notes: Largest community college in Virginia; soccer program benefits from the enormous and diverse Northern Virginia soccer market, including large immigrant communities.

name: Tidewater Community College
notes: Hampton Roads area JUCO with an active soccer program; draws from the large military-affiliated and civilian Hampton Roads soccer community.

name: South Plains College
notes: West Texas JUCO with a competitive soccer program; well-known athletics department and a good transfer pathway to Texas four-year schools.

name: McLennan Community College
notes: Central Texas JUCO in the Baylor University hometown; competitive soccer with strong coaching staff and good transfer outcomes.

name: Blinn College
notes: One of Texas's most prominent JUCOs; strong soccer program with multiple Texas/national tournament appearances and a major pathway to University of Texas and other Big 12 schools.

name: Navarro College
notes: Famous for its football program (Netflix's Last Chance U), Navarro also has competitive soccer; draws from the Dallas-Fort Worth metro area.

name: Cisco College
notes: Small West Texas JUCO with active soccer; provides a development pathway for players in rural Texas and surrounding states.

name: Northeast Texas Community College
notes: East Texas JUCO with a growing soccer program; serves the rural northeast Texas soccer market and draws from the Texas-Arkansas border region.

name: Panola College
notes: East Texas JUCO with competitive soccer; provides pathway for players in the Piney Woods region of Texas.

name: Paris Junior College
notes: Northeast Texas JUCO with active soccer programs; draws from the Lamar County area and recruits regionally throughout northeast Texas.

name: Ranger College
notes: Very small rural Texas JUCO; offers soccer scholarships and provides an opportunity for players who need a smaller, more personal environment.

name: Odessa College
notes: Permian Basin JUCO with a competitive soccer program; draws from a strong West Texas soccer market and has a track record of transfers to D1 programs.

name: Grayson College
notes: North Texas JUCO near the Oklahoma border; competitive soccer program that draws heavily from the Dallas-Fort Worth metro area and southern Oklahoma.

name: Arizona Western College
notes: Southwest Arizona JUCO with a competitive soccer program; benefits from proximity to Mexico and recruits heavily from the US-Mexico border region.

name: Pima Community College
notes: Large Tucson JUCO with active soccer programs; draws from a diverse Tucson market and benefits from the University of Arizona soccer culture.

name: Chandler-Gilbert Community College
notes: East Valley Phoenix metro JUCO with competitive soccer; benefits from one of the fastest-growing soccer markets in the Sun Belt.

name: Mesa Community College
notes: One of the largest community colleges in the US; strong soccer program in the Phoenix metro area with consistent NJCAA regional competition.

name: Eastern Arizona College
notes: Rural southeastern Arizona JUCO with active soccer; draws from local and international populations and has a tight-knit athletic community.

name: Yavapai College
notes: Central Arizona JUCO in a growing Prescott market; soccer program draws from the quad-city region of central Arizona.

name: El Camino College
notes: South Bay Los Angeles JUCO; California community colleges do not offer athletic scholarships but compete at high CCCAA levels. Strong pipeline to Cal State and UC schools.

name: Santa Monica College
notes: Highly regarded Los Angeles-area JUCO; no athletic scholarships (California CCs), but excellent transfer pathway to UCLA and other UC/CSU campuses.

name: Fullerton College
notes: Orange County JUCO with historically competitive soccer; located in one of California's most active youth soccer regions.

name: Golden West College
notes: Coastal Orange County JUCO with competitive soccer; draws from an affluent, soccer-active beachside community.

name: Riverside City College
notes: Inland Empire JUCO with active soccer programs; draws from a large, diverse Riverside County soccer community.

name: College of the Canyons
notes: Santa Clarita Valley JUCO in a rapidly growing suburb north of Los Angeles; strong soccer program with active recruiting in the San Fernando Valley area.

name: Moorpark College
notes: Ventura County JUCO with competitive soccer; strong local soccer culture and a solid transfer pathway to Cal State Channel Islands and other four-year programs.

name: Ventura College
notes: Coastal Ventura County JUCO with active soccer; serves the Ventura County market between Los Angeles and Santa Barbara.

name: Santa Barbara City College
notes: Nationally recognized JUCO with excellent academics and strong athletics; soccer program benefits from the UCSB athletic ecosystem nearby.

name: Fresno City College
notes: Central Valley JUCO in Fresno; strong soccer program reflecting the region's large Latino soccer culture and competitive Central California AA competition.

name: Diablo Valley College
notes: East Bay JUCO with historically competitive soccer; draws from the Contra Costa County market and serves as a transfer bridge to UC Berkeley and other Bay Area universities.

name: Contra Costa College
notes: West Contra Costa County JUCO with active soccer; draws from a diverse urban East Bay community with a strong soccer tradition.

name: Sacramento City College
notes: Urban Sacramento JUCO with competitive soccer; draws from the diverse Sacramento metro area and serves as a feeder to Sacramento State and UC Davis.

name: American River College
notes: One of the largest JUCOs in California; consistently competitive soccer program in the Sacramento metro area with strong transfer outcomes.

name: Cosumnes River College
notes: South Sacramento JUCO with active soccer; serves the Elk Grove corridor and provides a pathway to four-year programs in the Sacramento valley.

name: San Joaquin Delta College
notes: Central Valley JUCO in Stockton with competitive soccer; draws from a diverse San Joaquin Valley market with a strong youth soccer infrastructure.

name: Modesto Junior College
notes: Historic California JUCO with strong athletics; soccer program draws from the Stanislaus County area's large soccer-playing population.

name: Merced College
notes: Central Valley JUCO near UC Merced; soccer program draws from the Merced County agricultural community which has a strong soccer culture.

name: Reedley College
notes: Fresno County JUCO in the San Joaquin Valley; soccer program serves the Kings River area and the broader Central Valley agricultural community.

name: College of the Sequoias
notes: Tulare County JUCO in Visalia; soccer program reflects the large and soccer-passionate Central Valley Latino community.

name: Bakersfield College
notes: Kern County JUCO in Bakersfield; competitive soccer program that draws from one of California's most soccer-active inland communities.

name: Mt. San Antonio College
notes: One of the largest community colleges in the US; nationally recognized athletics program with a highly competitive soccer program drawing from the San Gabriel Valley and greater LA area.
